title: Laboratory Information System Officer (LISO) PRIT
description:
JOB PURPOSE:

The Laboratory Information System Officer (LISO) role is pivotal in providing technical and administrative support in Information Technology to Laboratory Medicine and Pathology. This position assists in implementing organizational and departmental policies and oversees the entire test process, from pre-analytical to post-analytical stages.


MAJOR DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:

Specific:

  • Possess comprehensive knowledge of laboratory information systems, equipment software, and other relevant software.
  • Optimize information systems to align with laboratory performance indicators.
  • Aid in the implementation and upkeep of the laboratory information system (LIS), including test reconciliation from various interfaces.
  • Provide technical support to all laboratory sections, ensuring adherence to expected turn-around-times, especially during IT breakdowns and downtime.
  • Ensure personnel compliance with policies and guidelines, collaborating with the Head of Clinical Laboratory and reporting officers on disciplinary measures.
  • Report and investigate IT system incidents, implementing necessary corrective and preventive actions.
  • Maintain the laboratory intranet website, dashboard, and in-house development software or applications.
  • Conduct training, competency assessments, and supervision of medical laboratory technologists and medical officers.
  • Draft and review documents such as policies, work instructions, bench guides, and forms.
  • Identify and troubleshoot major issues, deciding on escalation to higher authorities when necessary.

General:

  • Execute duties in a professional manner, commensurate with academic and technical skills and training.
  • Adhere to laws, regulations, and hospital procedures and policies.

JOB REQUIREMENTS:

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent. Or Diploma with relevant experience and competency.
  • Minimum of four years of experience in a clinical laboratory, including leadership or supervisory positions.
  • Experience in a healthcare setting certified by recognized accreditation body.
  • Experience in training and assessing personnel.
  • Proficiency in SQL scripting and data extraction with Oracle database.
  • Experience in writing and implementing policies and work instructions.
  • Familiarity with Ministry of Health licensing, and CAP, ISO, or JCI accreditation processes.
  • ASCPi certificate is preferred.

Interested candidate who wish to apply for the advertised position, please click on “Apply Now”. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.


Job Code: PRIT

EA License No. 01C4394

postedCompany.description:
PERSOL is Asia-Pacific’s leading Staffing and HR solutions partner, operating across 13 markets with deep local insight and regional scale. With more than 80 offices and decades of experience, we deliver integrated workforce solutions that are tailored, tech-enabled, and designed for the dynamic world of work.

We combine human expertise with smart technology to help organisations solve workforce challenges, unlock potential, and stay ahead of change. From recruitment and talent management to workforce strategy and advisory, our collaborative approach puts your goals at the centre.

In 2025, we came together under the PERSOL name — reflecting our bold vision for the future of work and our Group’s Vision: Work and Smile.

Whether you’re building teams, growing careers, or transforming how work gets done, we’re here.
